bead.title: build(feat-009): Cypher QueryStore over StorageAdapter
bead.description: Implement the production QueryStore implementation for axon-cypher using axon-storage::StorageAdapter primitives. The implementation must use portable get/range_scan/index_lookup/link primitives only and must not introduce SQLite- or PostgreSQL-specific query operators.
bead.acceptance: AC1. axon-cypher has a StorageAdapter-backed QueryStore implementation available to callers. AC2. Entity label/property reads use StorageAdapter index_lookup or bounded range_scan according to the planner operator. AC3. Relationship expansion and EXISTS probes use StorageAdapter link primitives or the dedicated portable link API, not backend-specific SQL/JSON operators. AC4. Errors from storage are mapped into CypherError without unwrap() in library code. AC5. Unit tests cover scan, index lookup, expand, incoming/outgoing links, and EXISTS probes through a fake or memory StorageAdapter. AC6. cargo test -p axon-cypher and cargo clippy -p axon-cypher --all-targets -- -D warnings pass.

events.body:
REQUEST_CHANGES
Implementation is functionally correct and well-tested, but it deviates from AC1 (lives in axon-storage, not axon-cypher) and AC4 (storage errors are silently swallowed into empty results rather than mapped to CypherError::Storage).

{"rationale":"The bead is well-structured with a concrete description, explicit portability constraints, and six measurable ACs covering implementation, error handling, testing, and CI gates. Minor issues: (1) the conventional-commit type in the title is 'build', which conventionally signals build-system changes, not feature implementation — 'feat' matches the kind:feature label and the FEAT-009 spec-id; (2) AC3 uses a disjunction ('link primitives or the dedicated portable link API') without specifying when each applies, making it ambiguous for an implementer; (3) AC5 says 'fake or memory StorageAdapter' — if a canonical in-memory adapter already exists (axon-storage MemoryAdapter), the AC should reference it directly rather than leaving the harness choice open; (4) the dep axon-77f3a5ca is opaque in this bead — a short parenthetical noting what capability it provides would make dependency intent reviewable without cross-referencing; (5) no file or module path is suggested for the new implementation, which would anchor scope and prevent surface-area creep.","score":78,"suggested_fixes":["Change title prefix from 'build(feat-009):' to 'feat(feat-009):' to align with kind:feature label and FEAT-009 spec-id.","Rewrite AC3 to remove the disjunction: specify that relationship expansion uses StorageAdapter link primitives, and EXISTS probes use the dedicated portable link API (or define one approach only if the two collapse).","Rewrite AC5 to name the concrete test harness: 'Unit tests use axon-storage::MemoryAdapter (or a minimal fake implementing StorageAdapter) to cover scan, index lookup, expand, incoming/outgoing links, and EXISTS probes.'","Add a parenthetical to the dep: 'axon-77f3a5ca (StorageAdapter trait + in-memory impl)' so reviewers can assess the dependency without a lookup.","Add a 'target module' line to the description, e.g., 'Implementation lives in crates/axon-cypher/src/store.rs (new file).' to bound scope."],"waivers_applied":[]}
